items.briefing:
The Five Eyes intelligence alliance — which includes Australia — has issued a warning that AI-related cyber threats are expected to intensify within months. This is a timely reminder that AI tools, while useful, can also be exploited by attackers to craft more convincing scams and phishing emails. Separately, members of the Scattered Spider hacking group, known for targeting large organisations through social engineering, pleaded guilty on the first day of their US trial — a group that has previously targeted Australian-linked businesses.

As AI-generated phishing becomes harder to spot, be extra cautious with unexpected emails or calls asking you to verify your identity or click a link — when in doubt, contact the sender directly through a known number.

featured.title: Five Eyes warn AI cyber risks are rising within months
featured.quote: Five Eyes agencies warn AI cyber threats demand immediate board-level attention and business risk treatment.
